channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Alabama refuge is a paradise for birders and thousands of migrating sandhill cranes
item.description: Flooded cornfields near the Tennessee River in northern Alabama are a paradise for birders who flock to see tens of thousands of migrating sandhill cranes every winter.
item.dc:creator: Kristin M. Hall, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US Must Win AI Race: Experts Warn of China's Advancements
item.description: Experts warn that the US is falling behind in the AI race, while China gains ground. Learn more about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: For TikTok users, mourning, frustration and clinging to hope as TikTok ban looms
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. is inching closer and closer to a potential TikTok ban — with the nation's highest court upholding a law that's set to halt new downloads of the app starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Elon Musk’s Starship rocket explodes during SpaceX flight test
item.description: A SpaceX Starship rocket broke up in space minutes after launching from Texas on Thursday, forcing airline flights over the Gulf of Mexico to alter course to avoid falling debris and setting back Elon Musk’s flagship rocket program.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: TikTok refugees are pouring to Xiaohongshu. Here's what you need to know about the RedNote app
item.description: A rare wave of U.S.-China camaraderie broke out online in recent days as “refugees” from the popular short video platform TikTok poured onto a Chinese social media platform to protest a now-delayed ban on the service.
item.dc:creator: Fu Ting And David Cohen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: TikTok ban: 5 takeaways from Supreme Court’s decision
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled Friday that a law requiring TikTok’s parent company to divest from the popular video-sharing platform or face a ban was constitutional, siding with the government in a battle over free speech and national security.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: TikTok CEO thanks Trump for commitment to finding solution after court upholds ban
item.description: TikTok CEO Shou Zi Chew thanked President-elect Trump on Friday for his commitment to find a solution that keeps the app available in the U.S., after the Supreme Court upheld a law requiring the platform to divest from its China-based parent company or face a ban.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: 59 organizations reportedly victim to breaches caused by Cleo software bug
item.description: Unsurprisingly, spokespeople for Clop’s corporate victims are being coy about the extent of the intrusions into their servers, or refusing to admit any at all.
item.dc:creator: luke.hughes@futurenet.com (Luke Hughes), TechRadar

item.title: Bitcoin soars past $100,000 ahead of possible early action on crypto by Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The price of bitcoin topped $100,000 again early Friday as a pumped up cryptocurrency industry expects early action by Donald Trump when he's sworn in as president next week.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: La IA conquista el sector de la energía y los servicios públicos
item.description: Para 2027, el 40% de las empresas eléctricas y de servicios públicos desplegarán operadores controlados por inteligencia artificial (IA) en las salas de control, lo que reducirá los riesgos de error humano, pero aumentará las vulnerabilidades de seguridad de los sistemas ciberfísicos.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Python eats the world
item.description: In this week’s Python Report: Everyone’s favorite snake-named language is getting closer to being everyone’s favorite language, period. We also have a guide to unpacking the power of Python’s abstract base classes, and a tutorial for developers who want to use Cython to speed up their Python code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Lenovo to acquire Infinidat to expand its storage folio
item.description: Lenovo Group has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Tel Aviv-based enterprise storage provider Infinidat, for an undisclosed sum, to expand its storage offerings. The startup provides several offerings, such as InfiniBox, Infinibox SSA, InfiniGuard, and InfuzeOS.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple satellite patent takes big step toward stable communication
item.description: Apple has big plans for satellite services, a new patent filing confirms. It’s the latest nugget of information to roll down Apple Confidential mountain, a plan to make it much easier for devices to maintain connectivity as they move between satellites.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Shape-shifting AI Transformer homes will leave you wanting one
item.description: AC Future and Pininfarina unveil AI Transformer homes, merging sustainability and innovation. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says this could change the way we think about home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: HPE beats Dell and Supermicro in $1B AI server deal with X
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has signed a contract exceeding $1 billion to provide AI servers for X, the platform formerly known as Twitter, according to Bloomberg.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The journey towards a knowledge graph for generative AI
item.description: How does the journey to a knowledge graph start with unstructured data—such as text, images, and other media? The evolution of web search engines offers an instructive example, showing how knowledge can be extracted from unstructured sources and refined over time into a structured, interconnected gr
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The cloud cost wake-up call I predicted
item.description: For years, I’ve cautioned organizations about the hidden downsides of cloud computing. Ironically, I’m “Dave the cloud guy” who warns enterprises about the cloud. The benefits of the cloud’s agility, scalability, and innovation can quickly become a financial sinkhole without proper management.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 800,000 VW electric vehicle owners' data exposed by software bug
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson has the details on a Volkswagen data leak that exposed the personal data of around 800,000 EV owners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI experts tamp down hype, expect challenges for 2025
item.description: A trio of AI researchers and experts in Lithuania and England have tossed some cold water on the hype surrounding generative AI and predict that in 2025, companies and investors will have to lower their expectations regarding what the technology and large language models (LLM) can do.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Britain's leader unveils AI strategy, including vow to rip up red tape hindering AI projects
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government pledged on Monday to rip up the red tape hindering construction of data centers that underpin artificial intelligence while promoting its “pro-innovation” approach to regulating the technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TikTok app a ‘national security risk,' Sen. Mark Kelly says
item.description: “I imagine [there's gonna] be a disruption in the service here, you know, starting on the 19th,” Kelly told CBS News’s Margaret Brennan on “Face the Nation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation
